title: Dissolving an aluminum can in sodium hydroxide
description:
I've had several failures recently, so for lack of a better video, I threw this together today. About 25g of sodium hydroxide was dissolved in ~100ml of water. An aluminum can was dropped in and allowed to react, producing sodium aluminate and hydrogen gas. The hydrogen kept building up under the can and lifting it out, I should have poured the NaOH solution inside but oh well. 

I'm working on the carbothermal reduction of sodium sulfate to Na2S right now, my best yield is around 40%. I think I need more heat! The next video might be on making phenol, I don't know I'm lazy we'll see what comes next.

title: Reaction of sodium persulfate with halide salts
description:
In this video I react sodium persulfate with NaCl, NaBr, and KI. As the size of the atom increases so does the ease of removing electrons because the valence electrons are further away from the nucleus. An electron has to be removed from the halide to give the free halogen, so iodide is the easiest to oxidize and fluoride is the hardest.

The bromide and iodide were oxidized to Br2 and I2, respectively. With NaCl the was no apparent reaction. I couldn't smell and chlorine and there was no green color. There was a bit of bubbling but I think that was just decomposition of the Na2S2O8. Perhaps it needs to be acidified. 

Warning: Do not breathe in bromine or iodine vapors. Do not get bromine or iodine on your skin. Sodium bromide, potassium iodide and sodium persulfate are harmful when inges

title: Burning trimethyl borate: green flames!
description:
I made some triemthyl borate today and I thought I'd show it burning. It's interesting because boron gives flames a green color, and B(OCH3)3 is flammable. 

Word of warning to anyone who makes it, B(OCH3)3 hydrolyzes very easily in air. If the ground glass joints are not greased then it will leak out in small amounts and a white film will begin to cover the outside of your glassware and the area around the distillation apparatus. Protect the final product from moisture.

Also I know I spelled hydrolyze wrong in the video but in my defense I am a horrible speller. I'm also too lazy to fix it.

description:

All of the seals and internals are fluoropolymer, so you don't have to worry about corrosion. There's no oil so pumping water or solvent vapor is no problem! If you are looking to get a pump for filtration or vacuum distillation of medium boiling compounds (under 250C) then this is the pump for you! It's rated to go down to 50 torr although it can get a bit lower.

title: Preparation of Barium Nitrate
description:
Warning: barium salts are toxic and concentrated nitric acid is extremely corrosive. In this video I prepare barium nitrate by the reaction of barium hydroxide with nitric acid, a simple acid-base reaction. The yield is pretty good at 98% based on hydroxide.  

Organic reactions coming soon I promise.

title: Benzopinacol Preparation: a Photochemical Reaction
description:
Benzophenone dissolved in 2-propanol undergoes a reductive coupling reaction (pinacol coupling actually) in the presence of sunlight. The melting point of the product is variable, with literature values ranging from 170 to 195 C. Considering the chemicals involved and the conditions, I think a MP of 173-177 C is enough for identification. If I wasn't heating so fast I think the range would be sharper.

Reference: Organic Syntheses, Coll. Vol. 2, p.71 (1943);

title: Abderhalden's Drying Pistol
description: I demonstrate the use of a drying pistol.
transcript: lab in this video I'll be showing the use of abdur Aldens drying pistol a piece of glassware that is similar to but has many distinct differences from a vacuum desiccator it is made up of two parts the main body is shown here notice the inner and outer glass tube on the right the inner tube is where the sample to be dried is placed the outer tube is connected to the bottom and top joint a flask is placed on the bottom joint and a condenser is placed on the top here is the drying chamber it fits on the end of the other piece of glassware notice the ridge this is to keep the drying agent in the chamber it also has a stopcock to control the flow of gas out of the apparatus the joint is rather large in order to fit a large sample into the drying chamber I will now set up to dry something I've placed water in the bottom flask to dry the sample at 100 Celsius I have placed concentrated sulfuric acid in the drying chamber to act as the drying agent and I have placed a Liebig condenser on top because water is not that volatile you can put any liquid you like in the flask below to determine the temperature at which your sample will dry here I have run a line going to my vacuum pump to ensure that most of the air is removed the stopcock was then turned to contain the vacuum and the sample is left to dry under heating for as long as you deem necessary alternative to vacuum drying ovens because the sample is heated it can be dried much faster than in a vacuum desiccator at room temperature its drawback is that known as large samples can be placed into the drying chamber

title: Making Phthalic Anhydride from Phthalic Acid
description: I make phthalic anhydride from phthalic acid by heating.
transcript: lab in this video I'll be preparing phthalic anhydride from phthalic acid the beaker seen here contains approximately 60 grams of phthalic acid it's placed on a hot plate and the hot plate is turned out to medium-high heat as it began to heat up I placed a piece of aluminum foil in a metal pan and set it aside for later when I want to collect my product while that's going on I'll talk a little bit about the reaction for most carboxylic acids it is impossible to form the acid anhydride just by heating but phthalic acid is special because it contains two acid groups which are adjacent to each other the formation of phthalic anhydride actually forms a ring the ring is quite stable and this makes the formation of phthalic anhydride much more favorable compared to other acid anhydride because of this when you heat a phthalic acid to around 180 Celsius it begins to dehydrate giving off water and becoming phthalic anhydride eventually it will stop giving off water and completely melt signaling the end of reaction you can see water condensing on the walls of the beaker as this reaction takes place state water was no longer being emitted an evaporating dish was placed on top to contain the subliming phthalic anhydride the solid then melted and we poured it out onto the piece of aluminum foil to collect the product solidified and was broken up and placed into a bottle for storage to phthalic anhydride

title: Preparation of 1-bromobutane
description:
I prepare 1-bromobutane from n-butanol and hydrobromic acid.


Note:  I used 17.5 mL HBr, not 18.5. For some reason I typed the wrong number.
transcript: lab in this video I'll be preparing one bromo butane I will use eleven milliliters of an butanol 7.4 milliliters of concentrated sulfuric acid and two 17.5 milliliters of concentrated hydrobromic acid 50 milliliter round bottom flask adding 4.1 milliliters of concentrated sulfuric acid flask in an ice bath doesn't need to be kept cold but I just left it in the ice bath while I did this concentrated sulfuric acid the flask was lowered into my oversized heating mantle and a condenser was placed on top this is preparing for the reflux that will take place next and the heating mantle was turned on once the contents of the flask started to boil I started the clock and reflux the mixture for three hours heating and let the flask cool to room temperature you could see that two layers formed to the upper layer being our product I set the flask up for symbol distillation to recover the bromo butane the mixture was distilled until no more oily and cloudy drops came over after this the distillate separated into two layers and there was only one layer present in the boiling flask butane it was then washed with water hydrochloric acid water sodium bicarbonate solution and lastly water these steps aren't included because I have a feeling that all of my viewers know how to use a separatory funnel bottom layer was transferred to a vial containing some calcium chloride to dry it as you can see after a few hours it had cleared up completely milliliter round bottom flask and a stir bar was added unfortunately I spilled a bunch and got some calcium chloride into the flask product came over between 95 and 98 Celsius this boiling point is almost 10 degrees too low and I attribute this to my crappy thermometer given the properties of the liquid and the chemicals we put into the reaction there's really nothing this could be other than our desired product the final yield was seven point nine six grams or 48.3% the disappointing yield is a combination of small scale and me spilling a bunch into my heating mantle during a transfer step this has been the preparation of 1-bromo butane thanks for watching and don't forget to subscribe

title: Making a Choline Chloride/Urea Deep Eutectic Solvent
description: I make a liquid by mixing two solids, choline chloride and urea. This mixture is known as a eutectic, and in this case the eutectic temperature is 12 C, making it liquid.
transcript: lab in this video I'll be preparing a deep eutectic solvent first and hydrous choline chloride and urea were mixed together in a 1 to 2 molar ratio inside a glass vial the vial was placed on a hot plate turned to low heat the mixture slowly began to melt and once it was liquid enough I added a stir bar to help it mix together over the course of an hour nearly all of the solid melted and we were left with a completely liquid mixture temperature the contents remained liquid this is interesting because the melting point of urea is about 130 Celsius and the melting point of choline chloride is over 300

title: Making Diethyl Phthalate
description: I prepare diethyl phthalate from phthalic anhydride, ethanol, and sulfuric acid. Video clip of a chemiluminescent reaction is from a NurdRage video, “Make Glow Sticks - The Science.”
transcript: lab in this video i'll be making diethyl phthalate this chemical is used as a solvent in glowsticks first I measured out sixteen point two five grams of phthalic anhydride and transferred it to a 250 milliliter flask this was followed by a hundred and fifty milliliters of ethanol which was used to wash the funnel out a boiling stone was added a stir bar would also have been fine finally I added six milliliters of concentrated sulfuric acid over the flask I set up a Sox lit extractor which was packed with 15 grams of three a molecular sieves water was started to the condenser and I attached a drying tube to the top of it for two hours and distilled off the excess ethanol I recovered about a hundred and ten milliliters of ethanol before calling it good and stopping the distillation water sodium bicarbonate solution until it stopped bubbling and then water again the cloudy bottom layer was dried within hydrous magnesium sulfate finally I filtered off the drying agent the yield was fifteen point seven seven grams a 65% yield based off of phthalic anhydride because of its high boiling point I will distill it under vacuum in a future video I used too much drying agent - there were many transfer losses as you can see the droplets here in my waste and an incident with a separatory funnel that involved my product spraying onto the bench

title: Diacetone alcohol Synthesis
description:
I prepare diacetone alcohol from acetone using barium hydroxide as a catalyst. The product is interesting because it is both a ketone and an alcohol. I will use this in a future video to make mesityl oxide.

Procedure from Vogel's Textbook of Practical Organic Chemistry, Fifth Edition, page 802.
transcript: lab in this video I'll be preparing for methyl for hydroxy Penton to own better known as dye acetone alcohol I will make it by the base catalyzed condensation of two acetone molecules a thimble packed with barium hydroxide octa hydrate was placed in a soxhlet extractor through which was run 130 milliliters of acetone this should be left to run until the contents of the flask boil at greater than 100 Celsius 80 to 100 hours is normal time for this excess acetone was then distilled off vapor temperature went above 70 Celsius to 40 milliliters left in the Sox lid heating to 60 Celsius under vacuum removed most of the remaining acetone flask and vacuum distilled the product was collected between 85 and 95 Celsius at around 30 Torr a small amount of acetone water for run came over it's worth being noted that not all of the product could be transferred to the flask alcohol the low yield is because my sock slit was only run for 35 hours however all of the unchanged acetone can be reused

title: Reduction of Copper Oxide with Hydrogen
description: I made copper metal from copper oxide by reducing the latter with hydrogen. Oxides of less reactive metals can all be reduced using hydrogen and I will show this process with a more interesting metal oxide in future.
transcript: lab in this video I'll be reducing copper oxide to copper metal using hydrogen gas this method works on a variety of metal oxides and I will show a more interesting example in a future video first I'll show you my apparatus it starts with a 250 milliliter flask with lumen amande water above it is an addition funnel with sodium hydroxide solution this leads to a calcium chloride drying tube after that tubing leads to a fused quartz tube with it has some copper oxide powder in the middle the two vents to the atmosphere I also had a Bunsen burner below the tube to start the experiment I started adding sodium hydroxide solution to the flask it took a little messing around but eventually I got hydrogen to be produced a pretty constant rate to test that hydrogen was actually coming out I put a test tube over the output and lit it I through the apparatus before lighting it on the output I then lit my Bunsen burner and started heating the copper oxide with it the hydrogen reduces it to copper metal and is oxidized into water you can see the water collecting in the tube farther on I'm not sure exactly how long you need to heat it but I did this for about 2 minutes and that seemed ok I let the copper cool under a stream of hydrogen molar hydrochloric acid this dissolves any leftover copper oxide and leaves only copper metal

title: Purification of Toluene from the Hardware Store
description:
I purify some toluene, removing thiophenes and non-volatile impurities. Some molecular sieves can bring down water concentration to only a few ppm.

Distillation music from www.bensound.com
transcript: lab in this video I will be purifying hardware-store toluene most toluene contains some thiophenes which can't be removed by distillation I will use sulfuric acid to remove them I first measured out 250 milliliters of the toluene to this I added 25 milliliters of concentrated sulfuric acid the separatory funnel was then shaken well to wash the toluene this washing was continued with fresh acid until the acid didn't turn yellow after shaking this was another two times in my case distilled water drying over anhydrous potassium carbonate should clear up the toluene I got impatient and went straight to distillation the water can then be removed as a low boiling azeotrope leaving relatively dry toluene to be distilled over [Music] bottle of dry thiophene free toluene ready for lab use

title: Magnesium Nitride from Magnesium and Nitrogen
description:
I make magnesium nitride by heating magnesium in an atmosphere of nitrogen. 

The nitrogen generator is from Astral Chemistry, check out the video here: https://www.youtube.com/watchv=DlkpPYWhUI0&t=505s
transcript: video i'll be making magnesium nitride from magnesium metal and nitrogen gas ammonium chloride three point five six grams of sodium nitrite and about point five grams of magnesium i first added the ammonium chloride and sodium nitrite to a flask along with around hundred milliliters of water the mixture was stirred until everything dissolved chloride drawing tube which was connected to a fused quartz glass tube in the middle I placed magnesium chips although magnesium powder would be much better this led to a second drying tube and finally to a flask of water to monitor gas generation when I was ready I turned on heat to the flask nitrogen was slowly produced and eventually reached a good rate I let the apparatus purge for around 10 minutes to remove any oxygen after this I let my Bunsen burner and began to heat the magnesium it started to take on a black color a dark film then slowly spread over the glass magnesium very hot and let the tube cool under a flow of nitrogen some interesting patterns formed in the glass I was able to dislodge the chips with the a of a stirring rod the pieces are black and green but of white patches of magnesium oxide there also appears to be magnesium metal underneath magnesium powder would have worked much better I held a wet pH paper over the pieces as I added water magnesium nitride makes ammonia with water and the pH paper would detect any bubbling and a blue-green color indicated ammonia also noticed the milky color of the water from the magnesium hydroxide something interesting was observed when I tried to clean my tube with hydrochloric acid I didn't expect it listen to this magnesium silicide with the glass which reacted with acid to make silane my quartz tube was permanently damaged

title: Making 4-Hydroxybenzoic acid from PABA
description: I make 4-hydroxybenzoic acid, and isomer of salicylic acid, from 4-aminobenzoic acid, better known as PABA. I first convert it to the diazo salt using sodium nitrite and sulfuric acid, then I decompose that with heat and acid to replace the N2+ with OH.
transcript: lab in this video I'll be preparing the molecule that you see here for hydroxybenzoic acid this is actually quite similar to something else you might have heard of to hydroxybenzoic acid also known as salicylic acid now personally I find the process that I use in this video to be much more interesting than the product and that process is diazotization that process is where a primary amine reacts with nitrous acid in an acidic solution to produce this diazonium salt you see here and now this salt has a huge number of uses and you can make many different things starting with the diazonium salt but me in this video I will just decompose it to produce this molecule you see here also a quick word of warning diazonium salts can be explosive and obviously that's not something you want to happen in the lab so some things that can increase the risk of explosion are too much nitrite and working in not dilute enough conditions first I measured out three point four five grams of for amino benzoic acid to this I added 7.5 milliliters of water and slowly dripped in 5.5 milliliters of concentrated sulfuric acid able to add all the sulfuric acid I got this suspension that you see here at this point I decided to reduce my scale to one third of the original idea I mix it up and measured out one third of the total volume which you can see here I flask and washed it down with some water bringing the total volume up to around 20 milliliters I then measured out 0.6 grams of sodium nitrite in the test tube here and I added about 2 milliliters of water to dissolve it while I left that dissolving I set up an ice bath around my flask this reaction needs to take place between 0 & 5 degrees Celsius so I let it cool down as the sodium nitrate dissolved once I reached the right temperature I added the solution of sodium nitrite to the addition funnel and began addition I had to play with the speed a lot turning on and off but eventually got a good rate and could leave it going prepared a 50 percent by volume sulfuric acid solution eventually I added all the sodium nitrite solution to the flask and everything dissolved I then let it stir like this for about 15 minutes after that time I turned off stirring and let the flask sit here for five minutes I next put the acid solution on a hot plate and put the reaction mixture in an addition funnel above the flask I started stirring and gentle heating and slowly started dripping in the reaction mixture I kept it around 60 or 70 Celsius and you can see slowly bubbles started to be produced these bubbles are of course nitrogen gas the whole addition took about 10 minutes and it turned yellow and then eventually went a more colorless flask off and let it cool to room temperature I was then pleased to see that a bunch of crystals had formed and I proceeded to filter them off with vacuum filtration here which I left overnight to dry and got this in the morning I packed a bit into a capillary tube and took the melting point of it to be about 205 which is a little bit low but it's close enough that I can reasonably say that this is for hydroxybenzoic acid
safety: diazonium salts can be explosive if dry

title: Synthesis of 3-Pentanone
description: I prepare 3-Pentanone by the ketonic decarboxylation of calcium propanoate. Low yielding, but it works.
transcript: decarboxylation this is a reaction where two equivalents of carboxylic acid reacted high temperatures over a catalyst to give a ketone along with carbon dioxide and water its name comes from the fact that it is a decarboxylation that produces a ketone it just so happens that this reaction will also take place with these salts of carboxylic acids I believe this happens with all salts but it works best with calcium and barium ones this reaction used to be used to produce acetone in a reaction that is demonstrated by Nile red today I will heat calcium propionate to high temperatures to give three pentanone as well as a little bit of calcium carbonate I bought my calcium propionate online as a food preservative it is used to preserve bread and we don't around 18 grams and proceeded to add this to a large 50 milliliter test tube I did this because I didn't want to damage any of my nice flasks next I wrapped a distillation adapter with a generous amount of PTFE tape in order to make a good seal I then fit this adapter into the test tube and attached the rest of my apparatus this consists of a grue column a vacuum tank off adapter and a receiving flask the very column isn't necessary I just used it as an air condenser a water cooled condenser would work even better when I was ready I let my Bunsen burner and began to gently heat the test tube after a minute or so I began to see condensation on the glass with further heating vapours managed to reach the condenser receiving flask heating was continued until nothing more came over which took about 20 minutes let everything cool down to room temperature separated into two layers I removed the bottom layer with pipette and dried the remaining liquid using this setup I found the boiling point to be 101 degrees Celsius which is only one degree below the literature boiling point dividing the samples mass by its volume I found the density to be about point seven eight grams per milliliter given these various properties the product here is mostly three pentanone the yield was quite poor at only 13 percent while the yield can be improved somewhat it will always be on the low side for this type of reaction

title: Make Cyclopentanone from Adipic Acid
description: I make cyclopentanone, a cyclic ketone, from adipic acid. This is an example of ketonic decarboxylation. It differs from my previous video because it uses the acid and a small amount of base to catalyze the reaction.
transcript: lab in this video i'll be making cyclopentanone from adipic acid this is actually another example of a Katona decarboxylation but in this case the two acid groups are on the same molecule producing a cyclic compound the reaction uses a small amount of barium hydroxide as a catalyst my starting materials are 100 grams of a dybbuk acid and 5 grams of barium hydroxide octa hydrate the two powders were mixed together well and added to a 500 milliliter flask which had a thermometer well and a distillation adapter in it the adapter led to a condenser which worked when I hooked up my water lines properly my 1 liter heating mantle was turned on and the temperature of the flask ramped up slowly at around 150 degrees the acid started to melt and crystals of adipic acid is formed higher up on the flask some water distilled over over at around 260 degrees a small layer can be seen on top of the water with some difficulty between 290 and 295 degrees for the remainder of the reaction this took a few hours at this temperature there is a good rate of reaction and any higher will make a dip ik acid come over darken to a black color when I thought most of the acid was gone I turned off heating left than I thought and this will lead to lower yields I pipette it off a small bottom layer of water and added a few scoops of anhydrous potassium carbonate to the distillate this is a good drying agent because it not only absorbs water but it also reacts with any adipic acid present and pipetted off a bottom layer of water which had formed mantle with this fancy expensive cord the crude cyclopentanone was added to a 50 milliliter flask on top of this was attached a with a crew column which went to a distillation adapter a Liebig condenser a vacuum take-off adapter and finally a receiving flask first the come off was at ninety three point five degrees and this is actually an azeotrope of cyclopentanone and water this the temperature rose to 128 degrees and I started collection this was continued until 130 degrees when the still had temperature started to drop cyclopentanone this represents a 60% yield not too bad considering how much adipic acid was left in the flask from stopping too early

title: Homemade 1100 C Tube Furnace
description:
I build a tube furnace capable of reaching 1100 C (2000 F) for $30 using parts from the internet and the hardware store. My design was heavily inspired by garage chemist and the following thread on SM: 
http://www.sciencemadness.org/talk/viewthread.php?tid=9705
transcript: building a tube furnace the heart of the furnace is this ceramic tube it's rated up to 1500 Celsius and measures 25 millimeters on the outside 20 millimeters on the inside and 200 millimeters long I also got some 22 gauge nichrome wire around the tube I tried to keep the spacing as close together as possible but I was rather uneven and as you can see near the end the spacing was very bad this is okay though because I will fix it as I put on the mortar the mortar is this high heat mortar it cost about five dollars for a large tube and it's rated up to 2,000 degrees Fahrenheit or 1,100 Celsius I painted it on with a brush the first coat is a thin one to keep all the wire in place and after that I added a second thicker coat to completely cover everything here you can see it after everything is cured I also added on these two solid copper wires to service connections as the mortar was carrying I made the body of the furnace four inch metal duct was caught a little bit less than 200 millimeters long and I kind of aluminum flashing to make the ends like this one you see here edge so I could fold it over and then clamp it with a hose clamp for insulation I got this ceramic fiber or ceramic wall which is rated up to 1,300 Celsius after wrapping insulation around the tube I put everything inside of the housing and clamped on the other end it took about one square foot of half-inch ceramic fiber in order to insulate everything well through the end and I might fix this later but for now it works a thermocouple inserted to the center I started my testing for the initial run I kept the voltage low at around thirty volts this is so I don't stress anything and allow any water that's still in the mortar to be driven off starting in the inside from inside reached 1,100 Celsius

title: Benzamide Preparation from Benzoic Acid
description:
I prepare benzamide using benzoic acid and urea, using a catalyst of boric acid. 

Special thanks to the members who posted on this Sciencemadness thread: http://www.sciencemadness.org/talk/viewthread.php?tid=4201&page=4
transcript: welcome back to the lab today I'll be preparing Benza mind in aromatic amide of benzoic acid and ammonia as well as the reaction of benzoyl chloride with ammonia for today I'll react benzoic acid and urea with a boric acid catalyst to make bends amide bends mine doesn't have a large number of uses but I intend to use it to make aniline through the Hofmann rearrangement this opens up a pathway to an alene without having to go through nitro benzene I started off with 110 grams of benzoic acid to this I added one hundred and sixty two point five grams of urea five grams of boric acid it was then shaken well to mix the reactants evenly the flask was placed in a salt bath on top of a hot plate I put a thermometer in the middle of the mixture and cover the top with tinfoil the thermometer allows me to take the temperature of the milk everything melted into a clear liquid around 130 degrees with evolution of gas eventually it reached about 180 degrees Celsius and I just did the hot plate to try and keep it at this temperature it should be kept around this temperature for about two-and-a-half hours notice the solids inside which will have to be knocked down periodically the reaction was rather uneventful but eventually it finished and I took it off and let it cool it gave the solid you see here to isolate the Ben's mind I added about 150 milliliters of 5% ammonia solution I then heated it with stirring on my hot plate the ammonia will react to make the benzoic acid and boric acid that may be present more soluble upon reaching 95 degrees almost everything had dissolved and I let it cool crystals precipitated as it cooled liquid was carefully poured off of these crystals 50 milliliters of a dilute ammonia solution products remained so I poured off the liquid into another beaker upon cooling to room temperature over an hour and a half or so you can see the very nice crystals which formed here the liquid was poured off and fresh distilled water was added the crystals were mixed well with the water to wash them everything was then vacuum filtered and washed with more water filter and left out to dry for a few hours eventually I actually melted them to drive off any remaining water my final product was seventy one point six one grams of some very nice Ben's mind the melting point of 128 degrees Celsius confirmed its identity the yield of 66% is somewhat low but it should be more than enough for the preparation of an amine

transcript: hello everyone and welcome back I hope you're all doing well I'm doing okay but unfortunately I have to phone in from home because of the current situation but I'm still excited because today I'm making something that I've wanted to make for a very long time cyclopropane is the smallest cycloalkane and it's rather unusual because of how strained it is most of its strain comes from angle strain and each carbon has four substituents and the farthest apart that four electron domains could be is 109.5 degrees so ideally the angle between the bonds would be 109.5 degrees but because it is three carbon atoms in a in an equilateral triangle the angle between the atoms has to be 60 degrees so that's very very far from ideal the carbon-carbon bonding orbitals aren't actually 60 degrees apart they are closer to the ideal angle 109.5 I think it's 104 degrees apart but that means that they can't overlap as much which makes a weaker bond and also because the the orbitals are farther out they're not not between the nuclei that means that the electron density is kind of following a curved path in the bonds and this is sometimes referred to as bent bonds which is very interesting cyclopropane was first prepared in 1881 by the verts reaction that's a carbon-carbon coupling reaction between two alkyl halides I put bromine here but that could be anything using metallic sodium and you can see if if these R's were connected to each other and the ends were connected then we could actually end up forming a ring and that's exactly what we do in the case of cyclopropane the reaction starts with sodium donating its electron the the bromine ends up breaking away and it leaves a single electron to create a radical on the terminal carbon then another sodium come along donate its electron and then the carbon can have a pair and that makes a carbon ion and as you can imagine this is a pretty good nucleophile and here is a partially positive carbon so that
